Please solve this issue: Multi-term search optimization logic inverted for large term sets

#### Description

When performing multi-term searches with a large number of terms that exceed `DisjunctionMaxClauseCount`, the batch processing logic is inverted, causing incorrect term batching behavior.

#### Steps/Code to Reproduce

```go
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2"
    "github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2/search/query"
)

func main() {
    // Create an in-memory index
    mapping := bleve.NewIndexMapping()
    index, err := bleve.NewMemOnly(mapping)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    // Index some documents
    for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
        doc := map[string]interface{}{
            "content": fmt.Sprintf("term%d", i),
        }
        index.Index(fmt.Sprintf("doc%d", i), doc)
    }

    // Create a multi-term query with more terms than DisjunctionMaxClauseCount
    var terms []string
    for i := 0; i < 1025; i++ { // Assuming DisjunctionMaxClauseCount is 1024
        terms = append(terms, fmt.Sprintf("term%d", i))
    }

    // This should create proper batches but the logic is inverted
    boolQuery := bleve.NewBooleanQuery()
    for _, term := range terms {
        termQuery := bleve.NewTermQuery(term)
        termQuery.SetField("content")
        boolQuery.AddShould(termQuery)
    }

    searchRequest := bleve.NewSearchRequest(boolQuery)
    searchResult, err := index.Search(searchRequest)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Found %d matches\n", searchResult.Total)
}
```

The issue occurs in the batch processing logic where terms are incorrectly assigned when the total number of terms exceeds the maximum clause count threshold.

Safer helper (recommended)
- Add a small batching helper so the logic is explicit and less error-prone:

```go
func splitIntoBatches[T any](items []T, max int) [][]T {
    if max <= 0 || len(items) == 0 {
        return [][]T{items}
    }
    var out [][]T
    for i := 0; i < len(items); i += max {
        j := i + max
        if j > len(items) {
            j = len(items)
        }
        out = append(out, items[i:j])
    }
    return out
}
```

} else {

    for _, b := range batches {
        subDisjunctions = append(subDisjunctions, newDisjunction(b))
    }
}

Safer helper (recommended)
- Add a small batching helper so the logic is explicit and less error-prone:

```go
func splitIntoBatches[T any](items []T, max int) [][]T {
    if max <= 0 || len(items) == 0 {
        return [][]T{items}
    }
    var out [][]T
    for i := 0; i < len(items); i += max {
        j := i + max
        if j > len(items) {
            j = len(items)
        }
        out = append(out, items[i:j])
    }
    return out
}
```
